results.rendered_html:
āvaha (von vah mit ā)
1〉 adj. f. ā herbeiführend, bewirkend; in comp. mit dem obj.: gandhāvaha  BRAHMA-P. 52,17 . bhayāvaha  ŚVETĀŚV. UP. 2,8 .  M. 8,347 .  BHAG. 3,35 .  R. 1,14,44 . 4,9,18 .  RĀJA-TAR. 5,344 .  VET. 5,9 . dharmā˚  ŚVETĀŚV. UP. 6,6 . malā˚  M. 11,70 . svargakīrtijayā˚  YĀJÑ. 1,356 . sukhā˚  BRĀHMAṆ. 2,5 .  R. 1,4,5 . 3,17,17 .  PAÑCAT. I, 463 . III, 5 .  HIT. I, 172 .  BRAHMA-P. 49,1 . jayā˚  R. 1,23,13 . hitā˚ 4,49,10 . viyogā˚  ŚĀNTIŚ. 1,20 . kṣayā˚  R. 6,74,39 .  PAÑCAT. V, 62 . kleśā˚  RAGH. 14,5 . duḥkhā˚  KATHĀS. 15,111 . śubhā˚  AK. 1,1,4,5 . namratonnamratā˚  RĀJA-TAR. 5,223 . hāsyā˚ 399 .
— 2〉 m. N. eines der siehen Winde  HARIV. 12787 .  BRAHMĀṆḌA-P.  beim Sch. zu  ŚĀK. 165 . einer der sieben Zungen des Feuers  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. I, 190, N.

results.rendered_html:
āvaha a. (As last member of comp.) Producing, leading or tending to, bringing on, kleśāvahā bharturalakṣaṇā'haṃ  R. 14. 5 ; so duḥkha˚, bhaya˚, kṣaya˚ &c.
— haḥ
1 N. of one of the seven winds or bands of air, usually assigned to the bhuvarloka or atmospheric region between the bhūrloka and svarloka.
2 One of the seven tongues of fire.
